extraction.one_line_summary: VALGREN Michael attacked solo with 48km to go, sustained the gap through cobbles/climbs to win by significant margin over VAN AVERMAET.
tactical_patterns: VALGREN sustained 48km solo breakaway, suggesting Omloop course lacks firepower concentration zones to force recapture of strong soloists.
TRENTIN attempted bridge to VALGREN but failed, indicating even experienced classics riders misjudged effort required for 40km+ chase.
Astana rode tactically despite VALGREN solo, implying team protected leader rather than committing to chase with gap controllable.

rider_signals.VALGREN Michael: positive: Won from 48km solo attack, rare endurance/power combination suggesting classics breakthrough capacity beyond one-day result
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: Failed bridge attempt revealed inability to sustain classics-winning power over extended distance despite team pedigree
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: BMC Olympic champion finished second despite pre-race favoritism, suggesting team tactics or form deficit allowed excessive gap development
